question: A process instance enters a business rule task and the internal DMN decision engine fails to evaluate the decision. What happens at the task?

options.text: An incident is raised at the business rule task; when the incident is resolved, the decision is evaluated again.
The task completes and propagates a `null` result variable so the process can continue.
The process instance is automatically cancelled with an error end event.
The token routes via a default outgoing sequence flow modelled on the task.

a.text: An unsuccessful decision evaluation raises an incident at the business rule task. When the incident is resolved, the engine re-evaluates the decision.

b.text: Failed evaluation does not silently complete the task with a null result; it raises an incident so the cause can be addressed.

c.text: An evaluation failure does not trigger an error end event automatically. It surfaces as an incident on the task itself.

d.text: Business rule tasks do not branch on a default flow when evaluation fails; the failure becomes an incident.

explanation: When the DMN decision engine cannot evaluate the decision at a business rule task, the engine raises an incident on the task. Once the incident is resolved (for example, after fixing the data or the DMN), the decision is evaluated again from that point.
